draft.introduction: Psalm 27 is a psalm of trust and longing for God’s presence, combining confident faith with urgent petition. Its central desire—“to dwell in the house of the Lord”—expresses the deepest aspiration of Israelite worship.

translation.text: The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The Lord is the stronghold of my life; of whom shall I be afraid? One thing have I asked of the Lord, that will I seek after: that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to gaze upon the beauty of the Lord.
translation.original_text: Κύριος φωτισμός μου καὶ σωτήρ μου τίνα φοβηθήσομαι

translator_notes.note: The desire to “gaze upon the beauty of the Lord” (τοῦ θεάσασθαι τὴν τερπνότητα Κυρίου) anticipates the Christian concept of the beatific vision—the direct contemplation of God as the ultimate human fulfillment.
